Definition: An individual or organization that holds an active or historical water service account with Arizona Water Company.
Purpose: Standardize the core party concept used across billing, service, and analytics.

Definition: The unique AWC billing account identifier in the format AWC-XX-###### where XX is the two-letter water system code and ###### is a six-digit sequence.
Purpose: Provide a single conformed key for joining customers to billing across systems.

Definition: The full legal name of the account holder. Personally identifiable information subject to GDPR and CCPA.
Purpose: Identify the responsible party while flagging the field as protected PII.

Definition: The physical address where water service is delivered. PII because it pinpoints a household.
Purpose: Locate service delivery and flag the field as protected personal data.

Definition: The customer's contact email. PII and the channel governed by marketing consent.
Purpose: Enable contact while enforcing opt-out and validity controls.

Definition: The customer's contact telephone number. Personally identifiable information.
Purpose: Enable contact while flagging the field as protected PII.

Definition: A boolean flag indicating whether the customer has opted OUT of marketing communications. When TRUE the customer must not receive marketing.
Purpose: Enforce GDPR/CCPA opt-out and prevent the wrongful-marketing breach.

Definition: The lifecycle state of a customer account: Active, Suspended, Closed, or Pending.
Purpose: Provide a controlled vocabulary that drives billing and service eligibility.

Definition: The segment of the customer: Residential, Commercial, Agriculture, or Industrial.
Purpose: Segment customers for rate selection and usage analysis.

Definition: The water system that serves a given customer, linking each customer to one of AWC's eight cataloged systems.
Purpose: Connect customers to the operational source of their water and to lineage.

Definition: A tiered pricing structure in which essential lifeline use is charged at the lowest rate and discretionary use at the highest, so the price itself encourages conservation.
Purpose: Standardize how AWC describes its rate design across billing and communications.

Definition: The fixed monthly service charge applied to an account regardless of consumption.
Purpose: Define the non-volumetric portion of a bill for revenue analysis.

Definition: The volumetric charge for water consumed within a specific pricing tier, calculated as gallons in tier multiplied by the tier rate per 1000 gallons.
Purpose: Explain how consumption converts into graduated charges.

Definition: The portion of a bill attributable to wastewater (sewer) service.
Purpose: Separate wastewater revenue from potable water revenue.

Definition: The full amount a customer owes for a billing period: base plus tier charges plus wastewater plus tax.
Purpose: Provide the authoritative amount owed and the basis for consistency checks.

Definition: The settlement state of a bill: Paid, Unpaid, Overdue, or Disputed.
Purpose: Drive the collections worklist and overdue alerting.

Definition: The calendar month a bill covers, used to assess timeliness and trend usage.
Purpose: Anchor billing records in time for timeliness and seasonality analysis.

Definition: The total volume of water consumed by a customer in a billing period, measured in gallons.
Purpose: Provide the consumption basis for tiered billing and conservation analysis.

Definition: The portion of total usage that falls within a specific pricing tier band.
Purpose: Explain how consumption is distributed across rate tiers.

Definition: A volume of water equal to 325,851 gallons, used to express annual system-level water usage.
Purpose: Standardize large-volume reporting at the water-system level.

Definition: One of AWC's eight cataloged operational water systems, each serving defined cities and drawing on a defined source.
Purpose: Provide the source-of-truth entity for service areas and lineage.

Definition: The water source classification of a system: Groundwater, Surface, or Mixed.
Purpose: Characterize supply sources for resilience and quality analysis.

Definition: The estimated number of people who receive water from a given system.
Purpose: Size systems for capacity planning and conservation targeting.

Definition: A designation of whether a system is under heightened conservation management.
Purpose: Flag systems where conservation messaging and rate design are prioritized.

Definition: The regulatory standing of a water-quality report: Compliant, Warning, or Violation, judged against EPA limits.
Purpose: Communicate regulatory standing and trigger remediation.

Definition: A measure of water cloudiness in NTU. The EPA limit for treated water is below 0.5 NTU.
Purpose: Monitor a key safety indicator and tie it to compliance status.

Definition: The concentration of lead in water measured in parts per billion. The EPA action level is 15 ppb.
Purpose: Monitor a regulated contaminant and flag exceedances.

Definition: A boolean indicating whether bacteria were detected in a sample. Any detection is a compliance violation.
Purpose: Flag an immediate public-health concern requiring escalation.

Definition: A built-in PDC property classifying how sensitive an asset is: High, Medium, or Low.
Purpose: Drive access control and discovery filtering for protected data.

Definition: A computed percentage summarizing the reliability of an asset across completeness, validity, consistency, conformity, and freshness.
Purpose: Give analysts a single trust signal before using data.

Definition: A system-generated notification about an account condition such as Payment Overdue, High Usage, Low Pressure, Quality Issue, Leak Suspected, or Conservation Notice.
Purpose: Standardize operational alerting categories for service response.

Definition: A user-defined metadata field created by an administrator to capture organization-specific information, with a fixed field type and a defined scope across hierarchies and asset types.
Purpose: Capture AWC-specific metadata that the built-in properties do not cover.

Definition: A governance asset that states an intent or rule of conduct for how data must be handled, carrying built-in properties such as Status, Version, and Effective dates.
Purpose: Document governance intent and attach it to the data it governs.

Definition: A governance asset that states a concrete, measurable requirement supporting one or more policies, such as a required identifier format.
Purpose: Define enforceable requirements that policies depend on.

Definition: The link between a business term and a data asset, viewable from the asset's Glossary tab and the term's Data Elements tab.
Purpose: Connect business meaning to physical data in both directions.

Definition: A typed connection between two business terms - Synonym, Is a type of A, Has A, Related to, In favor of, or Deprecated - that captures semantic context.
Purpose: Express how AWC's business concepts relate to one another.

Definition: An interactive visual graph of the entire catalog - data sources, glossary, policies, and their relationships - used to explore structure and locate sensitive data.
Purpose: See the AWC data ecosystem at a glance and find PII visually.

Definition: A keyword label applied to an asset, manually or by a business rule, that assists discovery and faceted search.
Purpose: Group and surface assets such as all PII or all compliance-review items.

Definition: A metric, alongside Data Quality and Sensitivity, that signals how much confidence users can place in an asset.
Purpose: Give analysts a confidence signal beyond the raw quality score.
